name: Grail Sword
description: The Grail Sword, also known by various names throughout Arthurian lore, is a powerful and mystical weapon deeply intertwined with the Holy Grail. Unlike Excalibur, which represents rightful kingship and earthly power, the Grail Sword embodies spiritual purity, divine judgment, and ultimate healing. Its origins are often shrouded in mystery, with some accounts claiming it was forged by angelic beings or within the mystical realm of Avalon. The blade is said to shimmer with an ethereal light, capable of cutting through any physical or spiritual barrier. Its wielder is often blessed with enhanced strength, heightened senses, and an unwavering sense of righteousness. However, the sword's power comes at a price; only those with a pure heart and selfless intentions can wield it effectively. Those who are unworthy risk corruption, madness, or even death. The Grail Sword is frequently associated with quests for spiritual enlightenment and the restoration of the Wasteland. It can heal the sick, purify the corrupted, and vanquish evil. It serves as a symbol of hope and redemption, reminding all of the potential for good within the world. The sword's presence often signifies a turning point in the Arthurian narrative, a moment of profound spiritual significance that shapes the destiny of Camelot and its inhabitants. Its power is directly linked to the Grail itself, waxing and waning with the Grail's presence and influence. The sword is more than just a weapon; it is a conduit for divine grace and a testament to the enduring power of faith. Legends say that when the time is right, the sword will reappear to aid a worthy champion in a time of great need, ushering in an era of peace and prosperity.
subtitle: A blade of divine judgment and healing.
origin: Forged in Avalon or by angels.

keyMyths.title: The Healing of the Maimed King
keyMyths.description: The Grail Sword is used to heal the Maimed King, restoring his health and the fertility of the land. This act signifies the power of the sword to bring about spiritual and physical renewal.

keyMyths.title: Galahad's Righteousness
keyMyths.description: Sir Galahad, known for his purity, is the knight most often associated with wielding the Grail Sword. His ability to wield it demonstrates his unwavering faith and commitment to good.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A foundational text of Arthurian literature.
sources.text: A foundational text of Arthurian literature.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Perceval, the Story of the Grail
sources.description: An early Arthurian romance featuring the Grail and related artifacts.
